rubric_en: The wrong way to solve the affordability crisis
rubric_zh: 解决高负担危机的错误路径

paragraphs.en: This article was featured in the One Story to Read Today newsletter. Sign up for it here.
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章曾刊登在 One Story to Read Today 通讯中。点击这里订阅。

paragraphs.en: This past January, in his inaugural address, Zohran Mamdani memorably promised to “replace the frigidity of rugged individualism with the warmth of collectivism.” In the parlance of the Democratic Socialists of America, of which Mamdani is a member, collectivism is a good thing. It is not meant to recall Stalin’s seizure of farms, which resulted in mass famine, or Mao’s Great Leap Forward, which also resulted in mass famine. American socialism today is different. The DSA still formally aspires to “popular control of resources and production,” otherwise known as seizing the means of production. Yet the New York City mayor’s attention-grabbing policy proposals—to freeze rents, establish city-run grocery stores, and pay for universal child care—are aimed at a more modest goal: socializing the cost of consumption.
paragraphs.zh: 在今年一月的就职演讲中，佐兰·马姆达尼（Zohran Mamdani）留下了令人难忘的一句话：要用“集体主义的温暖”取代“顽固个人主义的冷漠”。在他所属的美国民主社会主义者组织（Democratic Socialists of America）的说法里，集体主义是件好事。它并不意味着回溯到斯大林没收农场导致大饥荒，或毛泽东的大跃进也导致大饥荒。今天的美国社会主义并不一样。美国民主社会主义者仍然在正式文件中追求“资源和生产资料的全民控制”，也就是俗称的夺取生产资料。但纽约市市长提出的那些引人注目的政策——冻结房租、建立市营杂货店、并为全民托幼支付费用——目标却更为克制：就是把消费成本“社会化”。

paragraphs.en: “Consumer socialism” does not liberate workers from the exploitation of owners; it liberates consumers from the burden of prices. Although its advocates may claim inspiration from both the Great Society tradition of the Democratic Party and Nordic-style democratic socialism, consumer socialism is really a muddle of the two. The Great Society emphasized poverty reduction through means-tested programs such as Medicaid and Head Start; consumer socialism is meant for all. And unlike the Nordic welfare states, which are supported by high levels of taxation for all workers, Mamdani’s approach aims to raise sufficient revenue from corporations and the rich. Consumer socialism tries to have it all: universal social provisions without universally steep taxes. It retains, like other forms of socialism, a supreme optimism in the ability of state planners to shape markets. Where the old central planners failed, the new ones think they will succeed.
paragraphs.zh: “消费社会主义”并不会使工人摆脱资本所有者对劳动的剥削；它让的是消费者从高昂价格中解脱出来。尽管其支持者可能同时借鉴了民主党“大社会”传统和北欧式民主社会主义，但“消费社会主义”其实是两者的杂糅。“大社会”通过医疗补助（Medicaid）和“幼儿先导计划”（Head Start）等有受益对象资格审查的项目来降低贫困；而消费社会主义面向的是所有人。并且，不像北欧福利国家那样，后者由所有劳动者支付高税负来支撑，马姆达尼的做法则旨在从企业和富人那里征收足够的收入。消费社会主义想要两全其美：全民社保性保障，却不要求全民负担高税。它和其他社会主义形式一样，仍保持着对国家计划者塑造市场能力的极端乐观。过去中央计划者失灵之处，新的中央计划者却以为自己会成功。

paragraphs.en: Mamdani is only one of consumer socialism’s proponents. The newly elected mayor of Seattle, Katie Wilson, is a former transit organizer who campaigned on both universal child care and spending $1 billion to pay for union-built public housing. The leading candidate for mayor of Washington, D.C., is DSA-backed Janeese Lewis George, who also calls for universal child care and massive production of below-market-rate housing. (She is open to the idea of government-run grocery stores as well.) Mamdani, Wilson, and Lewis George have claimed the mantle not of Stalinists or Maoists, but of a different subspecies of socialist—the “sewer socialists” who ran Milwaukee for decades starting in 1910. They made peace with the capitalist superstructure and devoted themselves to good, incorruptible governance and reliable public infrastructure—sewage systems, yes, but also parks, libraries, and fire departments. In their time, they were skewered for practicing “slowcialism.” In a speech he gave to mark his 100th day in office, Mamdani labeled “our 2026 answer to sewer socialism” as “pothole politics”—doing mundane jobs, such as filling more than 100,000 potholes, because “government is not too busy, not too self-important, not too mired in paperwork to fix the problems of this city.”
paragraphs.zh: 马姆达尼只是“消费社会主义”倡导者中的一位。新当选的西雅图市长凯蒂·威尔逊（Katie Wilson）是一位前交通组织者，她的竞选主张同时包括全民托幼和投入 10 亿美元兴建工会参与建设的公租房。华盛顿特区的领先市长候选人是得到了美国民主社会主义者支持的简·路易斯·乔治（Janeese Lewis George），她同样呼吁全民托幼，并提出大规模建设低于市场水平租金的住房。（她也对政府经营杂货店持开放态度。）马姆达尼、威尔逊和路易斯·乔治都声称自己继承的不是斯大林主义或毛主义，而是另一支社会主义支流——“下水道社会主义者（sewer socialists）”的衣钵。这支群体自1910年前后起，在密尔沃基执政数十年。他们与资本主义上层结构“和解”，并投入到清廉高效的治理和可靠的公共基础设施建设中——不仅是下水道系统，也包括公园、图书馆和消防部门。那个时代，人们也讥讽他们搞“慢慢主义”（slowcialism）。马姆达尼在一场纪念上任100天的演讲中把“我们对下水道社会主义的2026年回应”称为“补坑政治”，意思是做些日常事务，比如修补十多万个坑洞，因为“政府并不太忙、也不自大，也不会被繁文缛节缠住，因而可以解决这座城市的问题。”

paragraphs.en: Sewer socialism is attracting renewed interest in America because it is too boring to threaten capitalism. Lenin despised its predecessor, municipal socialism, for much the same reason. In the late 19th century in English and German cities, socialist administrators operated public utilities such as gasworks, electric trams, and even city-owned slaughterhouses. This variation of socialism aimed to blunt the rapacity of capitalism rather than sharpen its contradictions and hasten the coming revolution. Under municipal socialism, Lenin wrote in 1907, “attention is diverted to the sphere of minor local questions, being directed not to the question of the class rule of the bourgeoisie, nor to the question of the chief instruments of that rule, but to the question of distributing the crumbs thrown by the rich bourgeoisie for the ‘needs of the population.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: “下水道社会主义”之所以在美国重新受到关注，是因为它对资本主义构成的威胁太小。列宁出于与此近似的原因，也同样厌恶它的前身——“市政社会主义”。在19世纪末的英德城市里，社会主义官员经营过公共设施，例如煤气厂、电车和甚至市有屠宰场。这种社会主义变体的目标，是钝化资本主义的贪婪，而不是加剧其矛盾并加速即将到来的革命。列宁在1907年写道，在市政社会主义之下，“人们的注意力被引向那些琐碎的地方问题，人们的关注不再指向资产阶级的阶级统治，也不再指向该统治的主要工具，而是转向如何分配富有资产阶级为‘居民需要’所抛下的碎屑。”

paragraphs.en: Lenin’s ghost would be similarly unimpressed with contemporary American socialism—too much democracy, too little murder. Still, there’s nothing small-bore about consumer socialists’ desire to overhaul the economy. In their view, high prices are not market failures but moral ones, the result of greed and corruption, which can be vanquished with the right intention. The ideal state is a kind of Lake Wobegon, where every price is below average.
paragraphs.zh: 列宁的幽灵对当代美国社会主义也会持同样的不屑态度——民主太多，革命性太少。尽管如此，消费社会主义者“改造”经济的愿望并非小器。在他们看来，高物价不是市场失灵，而是道德问题，源于贪婪和腐败，这些都能靠正确的意图予以铲除。理想状态应当是一种“沃比贡”式世界：每一样价格都低于平均水平。

paragraphs.en: Michael Powell: What Mamdani doesn’t know about tenants
paragraphs.zh: 迈克尔·鲍威尔：玛姆达尼对租户不够了解

paragraphs.en: One irony of consumer socialism is that it is better tailored to the laptop classes—which now form the backbone of the Democratic Party—than to the American left’s traditional working-class base. The upper classes disproportionately use child-care centers, while lower-income households rely more on stay-at-home parents and relatives. This is partly because wealthier people can more easily afford formal care services. But not every community may even want to send their kids to a child-care center. Some evidence suggests that Latina mothers prefer to have a relative look after their children, even when cost is no issue. Rent stabilization and controls apply to units, not occupants—which in New York has sometimes meant showering benefits on celebrities and on politicians, such as former Governor David Paterson and the late Representative Charles Rangel. Upper-middle-class meritocrats are generally not exhausted by capitalism. They are exhausted by the costs of rent and child care in desirable neighborhoods—bills that the preexisting means-tested welfare state would never have covered.
paragraphs.zh: 消费社会主义的一个讽刺之处在于，它更贴合“笔记本网课阶层”——而这部分人如今已成为民主党的骨干——而非美国左翼传统的工人阶级基础。上层阶级更倾向于使用托儿所服务，而低收入家庭则更依赖居家照看子女的父母和亲属。这在一定程度上是因为财富更高的人更容易负担得起正式的照护服务。但并非每个社区都想把孩子送到托儿所。有证据显示，即便不受费用影响，拉丁裔母亲也更偏向由亲属照料孩子。纽约的租金稳定与控制政策适用于房源，而非居住者本身，这有时就把福利“倾斜”给了名人和政客，比如前州长戴维·帕特森以及已故众议员查尔斯·兰格。上中产的精英群体通常并非在资本主义中“被榨干”——他们被的是住在理想社区里的高额房租和育儿成本所消耗，而这些支出在既有按收入审核的福利国家框架下本就不会得到覆盖。

paragraphs.en: Mamdani will probably fall short of implementing his vision: Budget constraints mean that buses are unlikely to be free, as he promised; his child-care proposal received funds to last for just two years so far; and New York, a city of more than 8 million people, will have, at most, five city-run grocery stores by the end of his first term. Despite his pledges to tax the rich, he is limited in his ability to do so, and although his recently passed pied-à-terre tax might raise $500 million a year, universal child care would, according to his own campaign, cost $6 billion a year.
paragraphs.zh: 马姆达尼很可能无法完全落实他的设想：预算约束意味着他承诺的“免费公交”很可能难以实现；他的托育方案目前只获得了两年资金；而纽约这座超过 800 万人的城市，在其第一任期结束时至多只会有五家市营杂货店。尽管他承诺要对富人加税，但其税收空间有限，而且尽管他刚刚通过的豪宅第二套居住权税（pied-à-terre tax）每年可能带来 5 亿美元收入，但根据他自己竞选时的估算，普惠托育每年将耗资 60 亿美元。

paragraphs.en: The natural habitat of consumer socialism—solidly left-wing American cities—imposes serious limits on its ambitions. Unlike the federal government, cities typically cannot run huge deficits year after year, and must quickly reckon with promises that cannot be paid for. In 2018, New York City officials rolled out a voucher program with an unwieldy name, CityFHEPS, and the laudable goal of decreasing homelessness by subsidizing housing. In 2019, the annual cost of the program was budgeted at $25 million; its projected cost last fiscal year reached $1.7 billion, as the number of recipients and the cost per voucher increased simultaneously. Faced with the city’s daunting budget deficit, Mamdani reversed his campaign pledge to expand CityFHEPS and has instead scrambled for ways to hold costs down.
paragraphs.zh: 消费主义式社会主义的自然土壤——那些坚定左翼的美国城市——对其野心也设下了严峻限制。与联邦政府不同，城市通常不能年复一年地大规模举债，只能很快面对那些无法兜底的承诺。2018 年，纽约市政府推出了一个名称拗口的代金券项目“CityFHEPS”，目标是通过住房补贴减少无家可归现象。2019 年，该项目的年度预算定为 2500 万美元；但在上一财年，其预估支出已飙升至 17 亿美元，因为受益人数和每张代金券的补贴金额同时上升。在面对纽约严峻预算赤字的压力下，马姆达尼取消了选举期间要扩展 CityFHEPS 的承诺，转而四处寻找压缩支出的办法。

paragraphs.en: Many risks lurk in consumer socialism’s promises of cheap goods and services. If New York City or Washington, D.C., rapidly increases subsidies for child care, for instance, without expanding the number of approved providers, the existing ones will charge more to meet excessive demand. Another complication is that the more generous any city benefits are, the more people will move across municipal limits to use them—creating a cost spiral.
paragraphs.zh: 消费主义式社会主义在提供低价商品和服务的承诺中蕴含着许多风险。比如，如果纽约市或华盛顿特区大幅提高托育补贴，却没有同步增加合格服务提供者数量，现有机构会借机抬高价格以应对过剩需求。另一个问题是，任何城市福利越慷慨，就越会吸引更多人跨越行政边界来申请使用，从而形成不断上升的成本螺旋。

paragraphs.en: The sewer socialists chose their targets carefully. Milwaukee’s mayors had a strong economic rationale for pursuing public ownership of utilities: avoiding the massive, duplicative costs of rival water networks without letting a private monopoly gouge consumers. In contrast, publicly run grocery stores are interventions in a low-margin, highly competitive industry. In his 100-days speech, Mamdani pledged, “At our stores, eggs will be cheaper. Bread will be cheaper. Grocery shopping will no longer be an unsolvable equation.” But the mathematics of running retail outlets might prove more flummoxing than he realizes. Many aspects of life in the Soviet Union have come to be retroactively romanticized; its grocery stores are not among them.
paragraphs.zh: “下水道社会主义者”在选定干预领域时很谨慎。密尔沃基的市长之所以主张公共控股公用事业企业，有着很强的经济逻辑：就是为了避免竞争水网带来的巨大重复建设成本，并防止私人垄断企业对消费者任意加价。相比之下，由政府直接经营杂货店属于对一个低利润、竞争激烈行业的干预。在他就任百日演讲中，马姆达尼承诺说：“在我们这里，鸡蛋会更便宜。面包会更便宜。买菜不再是一道解不开的难题。”但零售门店的经营数学逻辑，可能比他意识到的要复杂得多。苏联时代生活的许多方面如今被反向浪漫化了；其杂货店并不在其中。

paragraphs.en: Optimists think—or hope—that Mamdani-style socialists have a greater awareness of economic constraints than they let on. My colleague Derek Thompson recently said that Mamdani may be sporting “the abundance mullet, which is to say, economic populism in the front and abundance in the back.” Abundance is a term that Thompson coined in this magazine to describe policies that expand supply through public and private investment and deregulation—essentially supply-side economics for liberals. The idea is that Mamdani would win popular support by freezing rents—an idea almost unanimously derided by economists—while simultaneously boosting home-building. But the second half of this bargain may never materialize. Although New York’s Rent Guidelines Board has approved a promised freeze for nearly 1 million rent-stabilized apartments, Mamdani’s efforts to permit more home-building have been plodding by comparison. Absent new supply, restrictions on rent increases will limit mobility for those who receive the benefit, and increase costs for those who do not.
paragraphs.zh: 乐观主义者会认为——或者希望——马姆达尼式的社会主义者比表面上更清楚经济约束。他的同事德里克·汤普森最近说，马姆达尼可能是穿着“富足鲈鱼形象”——也就是前面是经济民粹，后面才是富足。富足一词是汤普森在本刊中创造的，用来指那些通过公共和私人投资、以及放松管制来扩大供给的政策——本质上是偏自由主义的“供给侧经济学”。这个想法是，马姆达尼会通过冻结租金赢得民众支持——这是一个几乎被经济学家一致嘲讽的想法——同时又能同步推进更多住房建设。但这笔交易的后半部分可能永远实现不了。尽管纽约市租金指导委员会已经批准了近100万套受控租金公寓的预期租金冻结，马姆达尼在放开更多住房建设方面的努力，相比之下进展得很慢。若没有新增供给，那么对租金涨幅的限制会让受益者的流动性下降，并把更高成本转嫁给那些没有受益的人。

paragraphs.en: Derek Thompson: A simple plan to solve all of America’s problems
paragraphs.zh: 德里克·汤普森：解决美国所有问题的一个简单方案

paragraphs.en: Plans are already afoot to scale consumer socialism across America. National Democrats have realized that the high cost of living makes for a powerful midterm-election theme, and some in the party prefer to address it with subsidies and price controls rather than measures that increase personal incomes and economic growth. The Congressional Progressive Caucus recently released its “New Affordability Agenda,” which includes plans to make child care a nationwide entitlement—staffed by day-care workers paid as much as teachers—and to create a new set of housing subsidies for rent and down payments. Progressives also argue that this can be financed without tax hikes on ordinary people, but through targeted taxes on plutocrats and corporations. Enacting any such changes nationwide would require many more votes than the caucus currently has. And the problem with implementing such plans at the local level is that the rich can always decamp to Austin or Miami, as some of California’s billionaires are threatening to do over a ballot measure that would take 5 percent of their wealth.
paragraphs.zh: 在美国推广消费主义社会主义的计划已经在酝酿之中。国家级民主党已意识到，生活成本过高是一次非常有力度的中期选举议题，而党内一些人更倾向于用补贴和价格管制来应对，而不是通过提高个人收入和推动经济增长的手段。国会进步党团最近推出了“新可负担性议程”，其中包括把托幼和育儿照护纳入全国性福利权利、让保姆和托育工作人员的薪酬与教师同等，并建立一套新的住房补贴来帮助租房者和支付首付。进步派还主张，这些举措可以在不提高普通人税负的前提下实现，而是通过对超级富豪和企业征收定向税。若在全国范围内实施这些变动，将需要远超该党团当前席位所能拿到的票数。另一方面，在地方层面推进这类计划也有问题：富人总可以迁往奥斯汀或迈阿密，就像加州一些亿万富翁正在威胁要这么做一样，而这正是因为一项提议会拿走他们5%的财富。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps cities like New York will refine a functional version of consumer socialism, in which subsidies are balanced with enormous supply-side expansions in the number of homes and child-care centers, leaving everyone better off. Perhaps capitalism can be appropriately fettered within the confines of a select few cities, inspiring a nonviolent, nationwide socialist revolution. That would be a remarkable triumph for Mamdani’s consumerist ideology, which wears the transgressive label of socialism but is born primarily out of anger over prices (and an innate American antipathy toward taxes). American collectivism may be, as Mamdani promised, warmer than rugged individualism. It will certainly be warmer than Soviet or Maoist collectivism. It could also be just as unworkable. And it will certainly be extraordinarily expensive.
paragraphs.zh: 也许像纽约这样的城市会完善一个可运作的消费主义社会主义版本，在这种模式下，补贴与大量扩张住房和托幼机构供给这一供给侧举措平衡搭配，使每个人都受益。也许资本主义可以在少数选定城市的范围内被适度牵制，从而在全国范围内以非暴力方式触发社会主义式革命。这将是马姆达尼“消费主义”意识形态的非凡胜利：它戴着越界的社会主义标签，但其出发点主要是对物价的愤怒（以及美国人对税收的先天反感）。美国式的集体主义也许，正如马姆达尼所承诺的那样，会比崇尚坚韧个人主义更有温度。它肯定会比苏联式或毛式集体主义更有人情味。但它同样可能一样行不通，而且一定会异常昂贵。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the August 2026 print edition with the headline “The ‘Consumer Socialism’ Trap.”
paragraphs.zh: 该文出现在2026年8月印刷版中，标题为“‘消费社会主义’陷阱”。
